title: Step 1: The Big Question — Why Are Plants Green?

frames.text:
🌿 Here's a question that stumped scientists for centuries:

Why do plants never need to eat food — yet they grow, bloom, and live for hundreds of years?
frames.speech: Here's a question that stumped scientists for centuries. Why do plants never need to eat food — yet they grow, bloom, and live for hundreds of years?

frames.text:
The answer: plants make their own food using sunlight, water, and air.

This process is called **Photosynthesis** — and it's the reason life exists on Earth.
frames.speech: The answer is that plants make their own food using sunlight, water, and air. This process is called Photosynthesis — and it's the reason all life exists on Earth.

title: Step 2: Inside the Chloroplast — Where the Magic Happens

frames.text:
Inside every leaf cell there are tiny green factories called **chloroplasts**.

They contain a green pigment — **chlorophyll** — that captures sunlight like a solar panel.
frames.speech: Inside every leaf cell there are tiny green factories called chloroplasts. They contain a green pigment called chlorophyll, that captures sunlight exactly like a solar panel.
frames.svg_html: <html><body style='margin:0;background:#0D1117;display:flex;align-items:center;justify-content:center;height:100vh'><svg width='340' height='210' viewBox='0 0 340 210'><rect width='340' height='210' fill='#0D1117'/><ellipse cx='170' cy='105' rx='130' ry='80' fill='#0A2010' stroke='#388E3C' stroke-width='2'/><text x='170' y='22' text-anchor='middle' fill='#81C784' font-size='13' font-family='sans-serif' font-weight='bold'>Chloroplast</text><ellipse cx='100' cy='85' rx='28' ry='12' fill='#1B5E20' stroke='#66BB6A' stroke-width='1.5'/><ellipse cx='155' cy='95' rx='28' ry='12' fill='#1B5E20' stroke='#66BB6A' stroke-width='1.5'/><ellipse cx='210' cy='80' rx='28' ry='12' fill='#1B5E20' stroke='#66BB6A' stroke-width='1.5'/><ellipse cx='245' cy='105' rx='20' ry='9' fill='#1B5E20' stroke='#66BB6A' stroke-width='1.5'/><text x='100' y='108' text-anchor='middle' fill='#A5D6A7' font-size='9' font-family='sans-serif'>Thylakoid</text><text x='100' y='118' text-anchor='middle' fill='#A5D6A7' font-size='9' font-family='sans-serif'>disc</text><path d='M130 140 Q170 165 210 140' stroke='#29B6F6' stroke-width='1.5' fill='none'/><text x='170' y='178' text-anchor='middle' fill='#29B6F6' font-size='10' font-family='sans-serif'>Stroma (fluid)</text><circle cx='72' cy='72' r='6' fill='#FFEB3B' opacity='0.9'><animate attributeName='opacity' values='0.9;0.3;0.9' dur='1.8s' repeatCount='indefinite'/></circle><circle cx='60' cy='90' r='5' fill='#FFEB3B' opacity='0.7'><animate attributeName='opacity' values='0.7;0.2;0.7' dur='2.2s' repeatCount='indefinite'/></circle><circle cx='78' cy='105' r='4' fill='#FFEB3B' opacity='0.8'><animate attributeName='opacity' values='0.8;0.25;0.8' dur='1.5s' repeatCount='indefinite'/></circle><text x='42' y='125' fill='#FFE066' font-size='9' font-family='sans-serif'>Chlorophyll</text><text x='42' y='135' fill='#FFE066' font-size='9' font-family='sans-serif'>absorbs light</text></svg></body></html>

frames.text:
🧠 Memory trick:

**Chloro** = Greek for *green*
**Plast** = Greek for *form/make*

So chloroplast literally means "the green maker" — it makes food using green pigment!
frames.speech: Here's a memory trick. Chloro is Greek for green, and plast is Greek for make. So chloroplast literally means the green maker — it makes food using its green pigment!

title: Step 3: The Equation — Sun + Water + Air = Food

frames.text:
Photosynthesis can be written as one beautiful equation:

☀️ 6CO₂ + 6H₂O → C₆H₁₂O₆ + 6O₂

Carbon dioxide + Water → Glucose (sugar) + Oxygen

And that oxygen? That's what we breathe! 🌬️
frames.speech: Photosynthesis can be written as one beautiful equation. Six molecules of carbon dioxide plus six molecules of water, powered by sunlight, produces one molecule of glucose sugar and six molecules of oxygen. And that oxygen? That's exactly what we breathe!

title: Step 4: Why This Matters — The Oxygen You Just Breathed

followup_questions.question: What would happen if all plants disappeared?
followup_questions.speech: What would happen if all plants on Earth suddenly disappeared?

followup_questions.question: How does photosynthesis happen at night?
followup_questions.speech: Does photosynthesis happen at night when there's no sunlight?

followup_questions.question: Why are some plants not green?
followup_questions.speech: If chlorophyll is green, why are some plants red or purple?

frames.text:
🌍 Every breath you take has a plant to thank.

Every animal on Earth depends on plants for:
• Oxygen to breathe
• Food (either directly or through other animals)
• Carbon dioxide removal from the atmosphere

Photosynthesis is literally the engine of life on Earth.
frames.speech: Every breath you take has a plant to thank. Every animal on Earth depends on plants for oxygen to breathe, food either directly or through other animals, and carbon dioxide removal from the atmosphere. Photosynthesis is literally the engine of all life on Earth.
